title: # Start Kernel Orchestration Start Kernel orchestration for the current task inside this Codex session. Execute immediately. Do not create or edit GitHub issues, pull requests, review comments, or issue comments. Do not inspect CI unless explicitly asked later. Backup-only GitHub Actions dispatch or...
summary_text: [phase:critique] [family:kernel-design] [provider_hint:codex] Top architectural gaps against your target `/kernel` design: - **No enforced 3-loop pipeline (`simulate -> critique -> repair -> replan`) before implementation.** Current prompt requires bootstrap + acknowledgement, then “continue task,” but does not mandate 3 full refinement cycles before coding: [kernel.md](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator/.codex/prompts/kernel.md#L20), [kernel.md](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator/.codex/prompts/kernel.md#L26), [kernel.md](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator/.codex/prompts/kernel.md#L30). - **No per-phase lane minimums (6 planning lanes, 3 simulation lanes).** Enforcement is only total active lanes + phase presence at least once, not counts per phase: [check-codex-kernel-prompt.sh](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator/scripts/check-codex-kernel-prompt.sh#L292), [check-codex-kernel-prompt.sh](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator/scripts/check-codex-kernel-prompt.sh#L294), [session_watch.py](/Users/masayuki/Dev/tools/codex-kernel-guard/src/codex_kernel_guard/session_watch.py#L301). - **No codex-spark-first simulation policy in `/kernel` flow.** `codex-spark` exists in the bridge, but `/kernel` bootstrap actually runs only Claude+GLM lanes; no simulation stage is explicitly routed to codex-spark first: [codex-prompt-launch](/Users/masayuki/Dev/tools/codex-prompt-launcher/bin/codex-prompt-launch#L561), [codex-prompt-launch](/Users/masayuki/Dev/tools/codex-prompt-launcher/bin/codex-prompt-launch#L563), [kernel-lane-bridge.mjs](/Users/masayuki/Dev/kernel-orchestration-tools/kernel-lane-bridge.mjs#L620), [kernel-lane-bridge.mjs](/Users/masayuki/Dev/kernel-orchestration-tools/kernel-lane-bridge.mjs#L974). - **Cross-model post-implementation quality gate is not required before completion.** Prompt asks for parallelism broadly, but does not force a final “different-model quality review then fix” gate before declaring done: [kernel.md](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator/.codex/prompts/kernel.md#L32), [kernel.md](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator/.codex/prompts/kernel.md#L52). - **Policy drift between global and repo-local `/kernel` definitions.** Global prompt explicitly requires `Codex + Claude + GLM` baseline and fail-closed semantics; repo-local prompt (which launcher resolves first) is less explicit about that baseline workflow contract: [global kernel.md](/Users/masayuki/.codex/prompts/kernel.md#L21), [global kernel.md](/Users/masayuki/.codex/prompts/kernel.md#L31), [local kernel.md](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator/.codex/prompts/kernel.md#L16), [codex-prompt-launch](/Users/masayuki/Dev/tools/codex-prompt-launcher/bin/codex-prompt-launch#L16). If you want, next step is to encode this target as an explicit state machine in repo-local `kernel.md` + guard-side phase-count checks (not just presence checks).
